name: ST variable (chatvar) write/read test
note: Tests TRG set-var scope:chat write, chatvar:: macro read-back, and condition operators against chatvars. Four groups — run each setup badge, then send one AI message to fire the condition rules for that group. Uses separate var names per group so conditions never bleed across phases. VALIDATED 2026-06-28: string write/read PASS, = operator PASS, numeric < PASS, is empty PASS. KEY FINDING: not-set never fires on chatvar references — ST's variable API returns '' for unwritten keys, so the engine always sees a value. Use 'is empty' or 'not-empty' for chatvar presence checks, never 'not-set'.

rules.name: T1a: Write string via set-var scope:chat
rules.note: Group A setup. Writes 'hello' to trg_stv_str. Fire this badge, then send a message to run T1b/T1c/T1d.

rules.name: T1b: Read string back via chatvar macro
rules.note: Group A read. Badge reads trg_stv_str via compose + chatvar macro. Separate from T1a so write and read are distinct steps.

rules.name: T1c: Condition = string value — SHOULD fire
rules.note: Group A condition. Fires when chatvar::trg_stv_str = 'hello'. Run after T1a badge.

rules.name: T1d: Condition not-empty — SHOULD fire
rules.note: Group A condition. Fires when trg_stv_str has any value. Run after T1a badge.

rules.name: T1e: Condition != string value — should NOT fire
rules.note: Group A negative. Should NOT fire while value is 'hello'. Error level — if this toast appears, the != operator is broken.

actions.title: T1e: should NOT fire [FAIL]
actions.message:
chatvar::trg_stv_str != 'hello' → FIRED
value: '{{chatvar::trg_stv_str}}' — != fired incorrectly or value was not 'hello'

rules.name: T2a: Write numeric string via set-var scope:chat
rules.note: Group B setup. Writes '42' (string) to trg_stv_num. Fire badge, then send message to run T2b/T2c/T2d.

rules.name: T2b: Read numeric back via chatvar macro
rules.note: Group B read. Tests whether getLocalVariable coerces '42' → 42 (Number) or keeps it as string. PASS if raw value is 42.

rules.name: T2c: Condition = '42' (string match) — SHOULD fire
rules.note: Group B condition. Tests string equality on a numeric-looking chatvar value. If this fails but T2d passes, the value is Number not String.

rules.name: T2d: Condition < 100 (numeric comparison) — SHOULD fire
rules.note: Group B numeric comparison. Tests that 42 < 100 evaluates numerically. If T2c fails but T2d fires, the value is being held as a number.

rules.name: T2e: Condition > 100 — should NOT fire
rules.note: Group B negative. 42 > 100 is false. Error level — if this fires, numeric comparison is broken.

actions.title: T2e: should NOT fire [FAIL]
actions.message:
chatvar::trg_stv_num > 100 → FIRED
value: '{{chatvar::trg_stv_num}}' — numeric comparison broken

rules.name: T3a: Clear var to empty string
rules.note: Group C setup. Writes '' to trg_stv_clr (a fresh var, never set to any value). Fire badge, then send message to run T3b/T3c.

rules.name: T3b: Condition is empty after clear — SHOULD fire
rules.note: Group C condition. The var was explicitly set to ''. Tests whether 'is empty' recognises a var set to empty string. Known bug area.

actions.title: T3b: is empty [PASS]
actions.message:
chatvar::trg_stv_clr is empty → FIRED
value: '{{default:(macro empty):{{chatvar::trg_stv_clr}}}}'

rules.name: T3c: Condition not-empty after clear — should NOT fire
rules.note: Group C negative. After clearing to '', not-empty should not fire. Error level — if this toast appears, 'is empty' logic is broken.

actions.title: T3c: should NOT fire [FAIL]
actions.message:
chatvar::trg_stv_clr not-empty → FIRED after clear
value: '{{chatvar::trg_stv_clr}}'

rules.name: T4a: Condition not-set on never-written var — SHOULD fire
rules.note: Group D. No setup needed — trg_stv_neverwritten is never touched by any rule. VALIDATED 2026-06-28: does NOT fire. ST's variable API returns '' for unwritten keys, so the engine never sees a not-set chatvar. Do not use not-set on chatvar references in production rules.

actions.title: T4a: not-set on fresh var [PASS]
actions.message:
chatvar::trg_stv_neverwritten not-set → FIRED
(var has never been written)

rules.name: T4b: Condition is empty on never-written var — diagnostic
rules.note: Group D. VALIDATED 2026-06-28: FIRES even before T4c runs. is-empty on a chatvar that was never written returns true because getLocalVariable returns '' for missing keys. Conclusion: is-empty = not-set for chatvars — there is no distinction. Use is-empty as the universal 'no value' check.

actions.title: T4b: is empty on fresh var
actions.message:
chatvar::trg_stv_neverwritten is empty → FIRED
(if this fires, is-empty = not-set; if silent, is-empty requires the var to exist)

rules.name: T4c: Write then clear — separates not-set from is-empty
rules.note: Group D step 2 setup. Writes 'x' then immediately clears to '' in one badge. After this, trg_stv_neverwritten is set-but-empty. Send a message — T4a (not-set) should go silent, T4b (is empty) should now fire.

actions.title: T4c: write then clear
actions.message:
wrote 'x' then '' to trg_stv_neverwritten
Now send message — T4a should go silent, T4b should fire
